senses.definition: to get extremely angry, upset, or frustrated and behave in an uncontrolled manner

examples.x: She throws a wobbly whenever things don't go her way.
He tends to throw a wobbler if his favorite team loses a game.
Yesterday, she threw a wobbly when she found out about the mistake in her order.
He threw a wobbler during the meeting last week, yelling and storming out of the room.
If they don't get the desired outcome, they might throw a wobbly at the negotiation table.

senses.definition: to display one's anger or frustration by  complaining or shouting, particularly for a while

examples.x: During the meeting, he tends to rant and rave about the company's inefficiencies.
She often rants and raves on social media when she disagrees with a particular viewpoint.
Yesterday, he ranted and raved about the poor customer service he received at the restaurant.
She ranted and raved for hours after receiving a parking ticket.
If he doesn't get the promotion, he will likely rant and rave about the unfairness of the selection process.
I'm afraid she will rant and rave when she finds out about the change in plans.

senses.definition: to show one's disagreement, complaint, pain, or anger by shouting or screaming very loudly and in length

examples.x: Whenever he sees a spider, he screams blue murder and runs out of the room.
She tends to scream blue murder when things don't go her way.
Yesterday, he screamed bloody murder after accidentally breaking his favorite vase.
She screamed bloody murder when she saw a mouse in her kitchen last week.
If they lose the game, they will likely scream blue murder and vent their frustration.
I'm afraid she will scream blue murder if she doesn't get the job.

senses.definition: one's tendency to suddenly become enraged due to having low tolerance

examples.x: He has a short fuse and often snaps at others for minor mistakes.
She tends to have a short fuse when she's under a lot of stress.
Yesterday, he had a short fuse and got into an argument with his coworker over a small disagreement.
She had a short fuse during the family gathering and ended up storming out of the room.
If he doesn't get enough sleep, he will likely have a short fuse and be easily irritated.
I'm concerned that she will have a short fuse during the exam due to the pressure.
She's got a reputation for a short fuse, so it's best to approach her with caution.

senses.definition: to become extremely angry, agitated, or upset

examples.x: When he heard the news, he went through the roof and started yelling.
Her boss's criticism made her go through the roof with anger.
He went through the roof when he found out about the betrayal.
Their parents went through the roof when they discovered the broken vase.
I'm afraid she will go through the roof if she doesn't get a promotion.

senses.definition: (of a person) very angry or upset about something unpleasant that has happened

examples.x: She was all worked up when she discovered her car had been towed without any notice.
He got really worked up after receiving an unfair treatment from his boss.
The customer became worked up when the restaurant messed up their order and refused to fix it.
The team captain got worked up when the referee made a controversial decision.
She tends to get easily worked up if she feels someone is being disrespectful towards her.

word_list.pronunciation: æz ɔːɹ ɹˈɛd æz ɐ tʃˈɛɹi

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation in which one becomes extremely angry, causing one's face to turn red

examples.x: Every time he gets criticized, his face turns red as a cherry in seconds.
She always turns red as a cherry when someone interrupts her during a presentation.
When he heard the news, his face turned red as a cherry with anger.
After the argument, her face was red as a cherry from shouting.
If he finds out about the mistake, he will turn red as a cherry in frustration.
I have a feeling her face will be red as a cherry when she sees the mess in the kitchen.

senses.definition: to do something that makes someone become extremely angry

examples.x: Every time he interrupts her, it makes her hackles rise in frustration.
The offensive comment they made always makes his hackles rise.
When she heard the false accusation, it made her hackles rise with anger.
Their disrespectful behavior made his hackles rise during the meeting.
If they continue to ignore her input, it will make her hackles rise.
I have a feeling their insincere apologies will make his hackles rise even more.

senses.definition: to suddenly become enraged and uncontrollably angry

examples.x: Every time he insults her, she sees red and can't help but respond angrily.
Whenever they interrupt him during a presentation, he sees red and loses his train of thought.
When she found out about the betrayal, she saw red and confronted the person immediately.
If they continue to ignore her, she will see red and demand to be heard.
I have a feeling their insincere apology will make him see red and escalate the situation.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who suddenly becomes very angry and is unable to stay calm

examples.x: Every time he interrupts her, she gets mad as a hornet and unleashes her fury.
Whenever they criticize his work, he becomes mad as a hornet and defends himself fiercely.
When she discovered the betrayal, she was mad as a hornet and confronted the person immediately.
His disrespectful comment made her mad as a hornet, and she lashed out in anger.
If they continue to ignore her, she will get mad as a hornet and demand to be heard.
I have a feeling their insincere apology will make him mad as a hornet and escalate the situation.
Whenever she receives criticism, she tends to fly off the handle and respond with anger.
He frequently flies off the handle when things don't go according to his plan.
When he heard the news, he flew off the handle and shouted at everyone in the room.
She flew off the handle yesterday when her computer crashed, throwing things in frustration.
If they continue to ignore his requests, he will likely fly off the handle and express his anger.
I have a feeling she will fly off the handle when she finds out about the mistake.

senses.definition: to suddenly lose one's temper and become extremely angry

examples.x: Whenever he encounters incompetence at work, he tends to blow a fuse and express his frustration loudly.
She often blows a gasket when her siblings borrow her belongings without asking.
Yesterday, he blew a gasket when he discovered that his car had been scratched by someone in the parking lot.
She blew a fuse during the meeting when her ideas were repeatedly dismissed.

senses.definition: to become extremely furious about something

examples.x: Every time he hears that song, he foams at the mouth and goes on a rant about how much he despises it.
She froths at the mouth whenever someone challenges her opinions, unable to handle differing viewpoints.
Yesterday, he foamed at the mouth during the argument, shouting insults and slamming doors in frustration.
She foamed at the mouth when she found out about the betrayal, expressing her anger with vivid and aggressive language.
"If they continue to ignore his demands, he will likely foam at the mouth and unleash a torrent of anger.
I have a feeling she will froth at the mouth when she discovers the mistake, demanding immediate rectification.
Every time he hears that comment, he gets hot under the collar and responds defensively.
She tends to get hot under the collar when her opinions are questioned during debates.
Yesterday, he got hot under the collar when he found out that his work had been criticized in front of his colleagues.
She got hot under the collar during the meeting when her ideas were dismissed without proper consideration.
If they continue to ignore his suggestions, he will likely get hot under the collar and voice his frustration.
I have a feeling she will get hot under the collar when she realizes her proposal was rejected without proper explanation.

senses.definition: (of a person) severely irritated or enraged

examples.x: Every time she sees the messy room, she gets fit to be tied and starts yelling at everyone to clean up.
He often gets fit to be tied when he encounters rude customer service representatives.
Yesterday, she was fit to be tied when she discovered her car had been towed without any warning.
He was fit to be tied after receiving a series of rejections for job applications.
If they don't meet the deadline, she will be fit to be tied and express her frustration to the team.
I have a feeling he will be fit to be tied when he learns about the unexpected price increase.
Whenever he finds out he has to work overtime without prior notice, he goes ballistic and starts yelling at his supervisor.
She goes ballistic if anyone touches her personal belongings without permission.
Yesterday, he went ballistic when he discovered his car had been scratched by a careless driver.
She went ballistic after receiving a wrong order for the third time in a row.
If they cancel his vacation plans at the last minute, he will go ballistic and express his frustration to the travel agency.
I have a feeling she will go ballistic when she finds out about the company's decision to lay off employees.

senses.definition: to become really angry in a way that causes one to act uncontrollably

examples.x: Whenever he sees people littering, he does his nut and starts lecturing them about environmental responsibility.
Yesterday, he did his nut when he discovered that his car had been towed without any warning.
If they cancel the concert at the last minute, he will do his nut and demand a refund.
